scope: Inventory the current enterprise posture after M19 and land the first shared M20 hardening artifact without forking the native M16/M18 contract.

first_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_worker_capability_catalog
purpose: make worker capabilities explicit instead of inferred from client-local logic
keep chat, vs_code, cli, workflow, and chatops on one native worker or session contract
give later M20 policy packs one authoritative capability inventory to target

multi_tenant_policy_packs: reusable enterprise policy bundles by deployment mode
policy-pack attachment rules for clients and workers
rbac_refinement: enterprise operator role bundles across UI, IDE, CLI, workflow, and chatops
clear separation between review, decision, and execution roles
dlp_and_secret_policy_enforcement: outbound deny or escalation rules beyond the current governed report redaction pass
export-time secret scanning and policy hooks on runtime-native audit, incident, run, and governed evidence export paths
credential-class policy hooks without client-specific branching
worker_capability_matrices: capability grant classes mapped to worker categories and environments
verifier coverage for capability policy drift
reporting_and_export_integration: downstream export profiles and sink mappings for SIEM, GRC, and ticket systems beyond the current shared workflow/chatops/CLI report envelope
export profile definitions by audience and retention class
stronger contract between audit or evidence exports and downstream governance systems

next_batch: Extend the shared enterprise reporting envelope into the remaining enterprise review and export surfaces, starting with VS Code and desktop Chat review/export paths.
Add export-profile and audience metadata on top of the governed report envelope without forking the native contract.
Add export-time secret scanning hooks to runtime-native audit, incident, run, and governed evidence export paths.
Keep Chat, VS Code, CLI, workflow, and chatops on the same native M16/M18 contract while doing it.
Do not introduce client-specific hardening branches.

second_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_policy_pack_catalog
purpose: make the first reusable role-and-capability hardening packs explicit at runtime
keep policy-pack attachment rules on the same native worker and execution contract used by all ingress surfaces
provide one authoritative policy-pack inventory for later RBAC and capability-matrix refinement
